notes.resin: Domestic PF/UF resin production capacity. Minimal oil pass-through. ~$0.20/m3 per $10 oil.
notes.electricity: Quebec/BC hydro-dominated grid. Near-zero oil sensitivity. $40-55/MWh (mid $47). $0.05/m3 residual.
notes.freight: Montreal 1,800 NM to Savannah. Mid TEU $1,350. +$40/TEU per $10 oil. $40/23 = $1.74/m3.
notes.labor: $80/m3 effective (high cost, high automation). Negligible oil sensitivity at plant level.

countries.drying_energy_type: Biomass / natural gas — RESILIENT (hydro grid base)
key_risks: Highest labor cost in Americas dataset at $80/m3 effective
Softwood lumber tariff political risk (USMCA periodically contested)
BC beetle-kill legacy reduces premium log availability
structural_flags: BENCHMARK LOWEST SENSITIVITY: $3.12/m3 per $10 Brent is lowest in entire global dataset
Hydro electricity provides near-complete oil insulation on energy costs
USMCA zero-duty access is decisive structural advantage
Short freight (1,800 NM EC, 900 NM WC) dominates cost competitiveness

notes.resin: Import-dependent on PF/UF resins. Moderate pass-through ~4% per $10 Brent. 18% x $370 COGS x 4% = $2.66, adjusted for partial domestic offset = $1.50/m3.
notes.log: Radiata Pine $50-70 (mid $60/m3). Plantation species. +1.5% per $10 Brent. 2.2 x $60 x 1.5% = $1.98 / 2.2 = $0.99/m3.
notes.freight: Valparaiso 4,500 NM to Savannah via Panama. Mid TEU $2,200. +$27/TEU per $10 oil (short Americas rate). $27/23 = $1.17/m3.
notes.labor: $35/m3. Moderate productivity 8 m3/worker-day. 1% oil sensitivity blended = $0.41/m3.

key_risks: Electricity at $80-100/MWh is highest in Americas subset
Panama Canal transit adds route complexity and surcharge risk
CLP -3% YTD provides shield but Chile inflation trajectory is uncertain
Resin import dependence from US/EU supply chains
structural_flags: US-Chile FTA provides zero-duty access — structural trade advantage
Radiata Pine plantation cycle is well-managed with 25+ year rotation
Solar electricity penetration growing, reducing medium-term oil sensitivity

notes.resin: Some domestic resin capacity (Arauco subsidiary). Moderate pass-through ~3.5% per $10 Brent. $1.20/m3.
notes.log: Eucalyptus/Pine $40-55 (mid $47.5/m3). Plantation economics. +1.5% per $10 Brent. 2.2 x $47.5 x 1.5% = $1.57 / 2.2 = $0.78/m3 (plantation cost insulation).
notes.electricity: Hydro-dominated grid. $50-70 (mid $60/MWh). Near-zero oil sensitivity. 200 x $60 = $12/m3. ~1% sensitivity = $0.12/m3.
notes.freight: Paranagua 4,800 NM to Savannah. Mid TEU $2,250. +$53/TEU per $10 oil. $53/23 = $2.30/m3.

key_risks: BRL -4% YTD provides strong shield but Brazilian monetary policy creates volatility
Port congestion at Santos/Paranagua during harvest season adds freight premiums
No FTA with US — standard MFN 8% tariff applies but no ADD/CVD
Eucalyptus plantation expansion competing with agricultural land use
structural_flags: Lowest CIF in entire global dataset at $201/m3 — eucalyptus economics are compelling
BRL currency shield at -4% is strongest in Americas after Chile
Hydro grid provides near-complete electricity insulation from oil
Eucalyptus 7-year rotation creates flexible and renewable log supply

notes.resin: Fully import-dependent on PF/UF resins. High pass-through ~5.5% per $10 Brent = $2.50/m3.
notes.log: Balsa/Tropical $40-60 (mid $50/m3). +3% per $10 Brent. 2.2 x $50 x 3% = $3.30 / 2.2 = $1.65/m3 (forest access diesel costs high).
notes.freight: Guayaquil 2,600 NM to Savannah. Mid TEU $2,100. +$28/TEU per $10 oil. $28/23 = $1.22/m3.
notes.labor: $15/m3. Low productivity 6 m3/worker-day. 1.5% oil inflation pass-through = $0.62/m3.

key_risks: Dollarized economy — no currency shield against USD-denominated oil price rises
Full resin import dependence creates high petrochemical exposure
Thermal electricity component creates moderate grid oil sensitivity
Political instability periodically disrupts port and road logistics
Balsa species niche limits product diversification to structural grades
structural_flags: Short freight to US East Coast (2,600 NM) provides logistics advantage
Dollarized economy eliminates currency risk but also eliminates currency shield
GSP status may reduce effective tariff rate
Hydro component of grid provides partial electricity insulation

notes.resin: Import-dependent but small-market procurement. ~3.5% pass-through per $10 Brent = $1.40/m3.
notes.log: Eucalyptus $35-48 (mid $41.5/m3). Plantation species. +1% per $10 Brent. 2.2 x $41.5 x 1% = $0.91 / 2.2 = $0.46/m3 (lowest log sensitivity in dataset).
notes.freight: Montevideo 5,200 NM to Savannah. Mid TEU $2,000. +$45/TEU per $10 oil. $45/23 = $1.96/m3.
notes.labor: $30/m3. Moderate productivity 7 m3/worker-day. 1% + UYU inflation pass-through = $0.55/m3.

key_risks: Small production scale limits bargaining power on resin and freight rates
UYU -2% YTD provides modest currency shield but Uruguay inflation remains elevated
Longer Atlantic freight (5,200 NM) vs Brazil (4,800 NM)
Limited domestic resin sourcing options
structural_flags: HIDDEN WINNER: best-in-class CIF at $190/m3 driven by lowest log costs and hydro/wind electricity
Eucalyptus plantation economics with 7-year rotation provide renewable supply
Wind/hydro grid decoupled from fossil fuel costs — structural resilience
No ADD/CVD — clean trade status

notes.resin: Fully import-dependent. Moderate pass-through ~4.5% per $10 Brent = $2.10/m3.
notes.electricity: Hydro from Itaipu — near-zero oil sensitivity. $35-55 (mid $45/MWh). 200 x $45 = $9/m3. ~0.5% = $0.05/m3.
notes.freight: Landlocked — double logistics: river barge Asuncion to Montevideo/BA then ocean TEU. Mid TEU $2,800. +$48/TEU per $10 oil. $48/23 = $2.09/m3. Barge segment adds $200-400/TEU.

countries.drying_energy_type: Biomass / Itaipu hydro — RESILIENT electricity, moderate drying
key_risks: Landlocked — double logistics via river barge then ocean vessel adds cost and transit time
Full resin import dependence with long inland supply chain
Low mill productivity (5-6 m3/worker-day) limits scale economies
Parana River water levels subject to drought risk affecting barge logistics
Limited port infrastructure at Asuncion for containerized cargo
structural_flags: Itaipu hydro provides cheapest electricity in Americas at $35-55/MWh
Landlocked logistics penalty ($200-400/TEU barge surcharge) offsets cheap inputs
Pine/Eucalyptus plantation expansion provides growing log supply
PYG modest depreciation provides small currency buffer

shipping.port: Asuncion (barge to Montevideo/Buenos Aires, then ocean)
